creator: marklmi26-fbsd@yahoo.com
text:
(In reply to Mark Millard from comment #0)

It turns out that just before

0xbfbf6d20 in ?? ()
1: x/i $pc  0xbfbf6d20:	svclt	0x00bf6ef0

is executed the r7 register happens to have the value 0x229ebba0 that later shows up in t->cs.number internal to truss for what it sees for the SIGSYS:

(gdb) info reg
r0             0x0	0
r1             0x0	0
r2             0x17c8506	24937734
r3             0x65	101
r4             0xbfbf6e80	-1077973376
r5             0xbfbf6e7c	-1077973380
r6             0xbfbf6e80	-1077973376
r7             0x229ebba0	580828064
r8             0x0	0
r9             0xbfbfe13c	-1077944004
r10            0xbfbf6e7c	-1077973380
r11            0x0	0
r12            0x17ef3b1	25097137
sp             0xbfbf6b78	-1077974152
lr             0x65	101
pc             0xbfbf6d20	-1077973728
fps            0x0	0
cpsr           0x80000010	-2147483632

ktrace may well get the same value but sign extend the lower 16 bits (even though svclt has 24 bits for the number).
